content.question: What are the medium-term (5-10 year) and long-term (15+ year) implementation and transition costs of reversing the 6/8-year track system in Hungary (analogous to Poland's 2017 reversal), and how do these costs affect the net policy calculus compared to maintaining the current structure?
content.reduction_path: Detailed cost-benefit analysis modeling classroom capacity constraints, teacher retraining needs, curriculum redesign timelines, and graduate labor-market pathways under both delayed-tracking and status-quo scenarios. Qualitative interviews with Hungarian school administrators and teachers about feasibility and bottlenecks. Comparison to Poland's 2017 transition documentation to estimate analogous risks in the Hungarian context.
